config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa kontrol, itu mudah berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan dan martabat orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang menghormati hak dasar setiap orang. Dalam praktiknya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika individu dilindungi, bukan dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse overreach and abuse. Public health, safety, and basic order do matter, but they should be balanced against constitutional rights, due process, and freedom of conscience, not treated as a blank check to override people. When we say "the greater good" without strong limits, it can become a convenient way to silence minorities or unpopular individuals. A healthy society is usually one that protects individual rights first and then asks people to contribute to the common good within those protections.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju jika kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas mutlak di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang seharusnya dilakukan adalah menyeimbangkan kepentingan bersama dengan hak dasar seperti kebebasan berpendapat, privasi, dan perlakuan adil. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, terutama untuk ketertiban dan solidaritas, tetapi kalau hak individu bisa digeser kapan saja, yang muncul justru penyalahgunaan kuasa, bukan masyarakat yang sehat. Menurut saya, masyarakat kuat bukan karena individunya dikorbankan, melainkan karena hak tiap orang dihormati lalu diarahkan untuk kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that framing has too much history of justifying censorship, discrimination, and government overreach under the banner of the "common good." Society does have real interests in safety, public health, and order, but those interests should be constrained by rights, not allowed to erase them whenever it is convenient. If individual rights are treated as secondary, minorities and unpopular people are usually the first to pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu longgar, lalu berubah jadi pembenaran untuk membatasi kebebasan berpendapat, privasi, atau perlakuan yang adil. Saya setuju masyarakat punya kebutuhan nyata seperti ketertiban dan kesehatan publik, tetapi itu harus dicapai lewat pembatasan yang proporsional, bukan dengan menempatkan hak individu sebagai hal yang bisa dikorbankan kapan saja. Kalau hak dasar tidak dijaga, yang paling sering dirugikan justru kelompok kecil dan orang yang tidak punya kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a default rule. From where I stand in the United States, that idea has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power in the name of the "common good." Society absolutely has legitimate interests in safety, public health, and order, but those interests should be limited by rights and due process, not allowed to override them whenever it is politically convenient. If we weaken individual rights first, the people who suffer most are usually minorities, dissidents, and anyone without power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
